input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values.

The most likely cause: the `BindingContext` is never set on the page. The XAML looks correct syntactically, but without assigning the ViewModel as the `BindingContext`, the bindings have nothing to resolve against.

A secondary possible issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says "properties have values," implying they're set before or at construction — so `BindingContext` not being set is the primary suspect.


I should provide a straightforward solution showing how to set the `BindingContext` in the code-behind or through dependency injection, along with a quick check for the `INotifyPropertyChanged` implementation as a fallback.
